request.question: Joseph Wolfe is the son of the late conductor of which orchestra founded in 1904?

title: RCA Victor Symphony Orchestra
Wratislavia Chamber Orchestra
British Symphony Orchestra
Belleville Philharmonic Society
London Symphony Orchestra
Chamber Orchestra of New York
Joseph Wolfe
Israeli Andalusian Orchestra
Connecticut Grand Opera and Orchestra
Guiyang Symphony Orchestra
sentences: The RCA Victor Symphony Orchestra, sometimes also known as the RCA Victor Salon Orchestra, the RCA Victor Orchestra and simply, the RCA Orchestra was an American studio orchestra founded in 1940 by the RCA Victor record label for the purposes of making recordings.
Originally established as a salon orchestra based at RCA Victor headquarters in Camden, New Jersey, the group was created by Victor staff conductor Nathaniel Shilkret.
The name later was used for free-lance orchestras, mainly in New York City, assembled as needed to make recordings for RCA Victor through the early 1960s.
Its players included those from the New York Philharmonic, the Metropolitan Opera, the NBC Symphony Orchestra, and other major ensembles.
The RCA Victor Orchestra recorded with notable conductors including Leopold Stokowski, Fritz Reiner and Leonard Bernstein.
A number of their recordings received Grammy Awards.
In the early 1960s the orchestra was disbanded when RCA moved much of its recording activity to Europe and established the RCA Italiana Orchestra at its studios in Rome.
The "Wratislavia" Chamber Orchestra – Polish string orchestra founded in 1996 by the outstanding virtuoso violinist Jan Stanienda who is the orchestra's concertmaster, as well as its conductor.
"Wratislavia" plays music ranging from baroque to contemporary, and uses the classic framework of a Mozartian orchestra, increasing the ensemble as the repertoire requires.
The British Symphony Orchestra (BSO) is a British symphony orchestra founded in 1904 and disbanded in 1940.
The BSO was reformed in 2016 as a concert symphony orchestra in the United Kingdom.
The orchestra works in partnership with the Mozart Symphony Orchestra who are based at Cadogan Hall, London.
The Belleville Philharmonic Society is an Orchestra founded in 1866 in Belleville, Illinois by a group of interested citizens.
It is the second oldest continuously operating orchestra in the United States.
It consists of three ensembles - an Orchestra, a Chorale, and a Youth Orchestra.
The current conductor for the orchestra and chorale is Robert Hart Baker.
Leon Burke III is the conductor for the Youth Orchestra.
The London Symphony Orchestra (LSO), founded in 1904, is the oldest of London's symphony orchestras.
It was set up by a group of players who left Henry Wood's Queen's Hall Orchestra because of a new rule requiring players to give the orchestra their exclusive services.
The LSO itself later introduced a similar rule for its members.
From the outset, the LSO was organised on co-operative lines, with all players sharing the profits at the end of each season.
This practice continued for the orchestra's first four decades.
The Chamber Orchestra of New York (officially the Chamber Orchestra of New York - Ottorino Respighi) is a professional orchestra founded by composer/conductor Salvatore Di Vittorio.
It was established on March 27, 2006, on the 250th anniversary of the birth of Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart.
Kelly Hall-Tompkins (also member of New Jersey Symphony) is First Concertmaster; Daniel Khalikov (also member of The Metropolitan Opera Orchestra) is Second Concertmaster.
Joseph Wolfe is an English conductor, the son of London Symphony Orchestra conductor late Sir Colin Davis and Lady Davis (the late Ashraf Naini)
The Israeli Andalusian Orchestra- Ashdod (Hebrew: התזמורת האנדלוסית הישראלית‎ ‎ ) is an award-winning Israeli orchestra founded in 1994 in Ashdod, Israel.

The orchestra is composed of around 30 musicians and lyricists mainly of Tunisian, Moroccan and Russian origin and features traditional Sephardic Jewish-Arab and Andalusian music and poetry, combining classical Arab-Andalusian and European instruments.
The orchestra's artistic director and head conductor is Shmuel Elbaz.
The Connecticut Grand Opera and Orchestra was a non-profit, professional opera company and orchestra founded under that name in 1993 and based in Stamford, Connecticut in the United States.

By uniting several organizations including the then-Connecticut Grand Opera in Bridgeport, CT and the Stamford Chamber Orchestra under the general director and conductor Laurence Gilgore, the opera company and orchestra came together as one organization.
The amalgamated company is often referred to as CGO&O.

Guiyang Symphony Orchestra (, abbreviated GYSO) is an orchestra founded in Guiyang, Guizhou province, China on February 19, 2009, Its inaugural concert was held on September 19, 2009 conducted by Artistic Directors Chen Zuohuang and Li Xincao.

The orchestra's current Musical and Artistic Director is the Italian-American conductor Rico Saccani, whose previous engagements include leading the Budapest Philharmonic Orchestra as well as many engagements as a guest conductor across Europe and Asia.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- If the `passages` consistently corroborate the context claim and are identity-aligned, append one brief paraphrased supporting sentence that cites the corroboration (keep the summary compact).
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

response.hop1_query: Joseph Wolfe is the son of the late conductor of which orchestra founded in 1904?
hop1_docs: Joseph Wolfe | Joseph Wolfe is an English conductor, the son of London Symphony Orchestra conductor late Sir Colin Davis and Lady Davis (the late Ashraf Naini)

Joseph Young (conductor) | Joseph Young is an American orchestra conductor.  He is the Assistant Conductor of the Atlanta Symphony Orchestra and Music Director of the Atlanta Symphony Youth Orchestra.

Sarasota Music Festival | Sarasota Music Festival is an American classical music festival held annually during the month of June in Sarasota, Florida under the sponsorship of the Sarasota Orchestra.  The Festival was founded in 1965 by conductor Paul Wolfe who remained director of the festival through the summer of 2006.  After Wolfe's retirement, conductor and composer Robert D. Levin became the festival's director.

Joseph Haydn Kammerphilharmonie | Joseph Haydn Kammerphilharmonie is an international chamber orchestra consisting of 20 young musicians.  It was founded in 2011 by Andrzej Berezynski, Pawel Zuzanski and Heinz Kroczek.  The orchestra's conductor and artistic leader is Andrzej Berezynski.  The orchestra concentrates on classical and romantic repertoire and gives several concerts a year.

Huntsville Symphony Orchestra | The Huntsville Symphony Orchestra is a symphonic orchestra located in Huntsville, Alabama.  The current conductor and music director is Gregory Vajda.  Vajda has been the conductor since the 2011-2012 season.  The orchestra's Resident Conductor is Joseph Lee.

Allan McIver | Joseph Allan McIver (17 January 1904 – 15 June 1969) was a Canadian composer, arranger, pianist, and conductor.  As a pianist he performed with orchestras in the Quebec region in his early career and was the longtime accompanist and arranger for Trio lyrique.  He had a long and fruitful relationship with the Canadian Broadcasting Corporation, serving as a music director, composer, arranger, conductor, and pianist for nearly four decades.

London Schools Symphony Orchestra | The London Schools Symphony Orchestra (LSSO) is a symphony orchestra with musicians drawn from students in London schools, and featuring professional conductors and soloists.  It was founded in 1951.  Its founder and original director was the late Dr Leslie Russell, at one time assisted by Niso Ticciati.  From 1990 until 2001 the artistic director was Oliver Butterworth.  The current artistic director is American conductor/composer Peter Ash.
response.summary_1: Passage evidence: passage [1] states Joseph Wolfe is "the son of London Symphony Orchestra conductor late Sir Colin Davis." Bridge entity: London Symphony Orchestra. Missing fact / next retrieval: confirm that the London Symphony Orchestra "was founded in 1904" (retrieve the London Symphony Orchestra page or a Sir Colin Davis biography that states the orchestra's founding year).
hop2_docs: British Symphony Orchestra | The British Symphony Orchestra (BSO) is a British symphony orchestra founded in 1904 and disbanded in 1940.  The BSO was reformed in 2016 as a concert symphony orchestra in the United Kingdom.  The orchestra works in partnership with the Mozart Symphony Orchestra who are based at Cadogan Hall, London.

London Symphony Orchestra | The London Symphony Orchestra (LSO), founded in 1904, is the oldest of London's symphony orchestras.  It was set up by a group of players who left Henry Wood's Queen's Hall Orchestra because of a new rule requiring players to give the orchestra their exclusive services.  The LSO itself later introduced a similar rule for its members.  From the outset, the LSO was organised on co-operative lines, with all players sharing the profits at the end of each season.  This practice continued for the orchestra's first four decades.

Orchestra London Canada | Orchestra London Canada was a professional Canadian symphony orchestra based in London, Ontario.  The orchestra was founded by conductor and violinist Bruce Sharpe in 1937 with the name the London Civic Symphony Orchestra.  In 1957 the orchestra changed its name to the London Symphony Orchestra, and it adopted its current name in 1981.

Cecil James | Cecil Edwin James (10 April 1913 – 13 January 1999) was a prominent English bassoonist born in London to a musical family.  His father Wilfred (1878-1941) was a bassoonist in the Queen's Hall Orchestra and professor at the Royal College of Music.  His uncle Edwin (1861-1921), also a fine bassoonist, was a founding member of the London Symphony Orchestra in 1904.  His uncle Frank was second trumpet with the BBC Symphony Orchestra.

London Philharmonic Orchestra | The London Philharmonic Orchestra (LPO) is one of five permanent symphony orchestras based in London.  It was founded by the conductors Sir Thomas Beecham and Malcolm Sargent in 1932 as a rival to the existing London Symphony and BBC Symphony Orchestras.

London Mozart Symphony Orchestra | The Mozart Symphony Orchestra, formally the London Mozart Symphony Orchestra, is a symphony orchestra founded by Philip Mackenzie and businessman Patrick Tame.  It is unique in that it never promotes its own concerts but is only available for hire by other organisations.

London Symphony Orchestra, Vol. II | London Symphony Orchestra, Vol.  2 is a 1987 album featuring Frank Zappa's music performed by the London Symphony Orchestra.  It is the follow-up to 1983's "London Symphony Orchestra, Vol.  1", and was recorded at the same sessions, in January 1983.  The two albums were later combined, and re-released on a Rykodisc CD as "London Symphony Orchestra Vol.  1 & 2" (1995).
response.summary_2: Joseph Wolfe is the son of the late Sir Colin Davis, conductor of the London Symphony Orchestra. The London Symphony Orchestra was founded in 1904 (passage [2]).
